# Testing Best Practices вҖ” Chat Exporter

> Verbindliche Referenz fГјr alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** вҖ” es enthГӨlt die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.

## Tech-Stack

| Bereich | Tool | Version |
|---------|------|---------|
| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | вҖ” |
| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |

## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing

**Lies nur die Module, die fГјr deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**

| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
|---------|-------------------|
| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
| **Code-Review durchfГјhren** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
| **Test-Kategorien wГӨhlen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
| **VerfГјgbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |

---

## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gГјltig)

### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung

Tests prГјfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.

```typescript
// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })

  const result = generatePreview(selection)

  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
})

// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
  generatePreview(createSelection())
  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
})
```

### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)

Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:

```typescript
test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
  // Arrange
  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }

  // Act
  const result = createSelection(overrides)

  // Assert
  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
})
```

### Given-When-Then (fГјr komplexe Szenarien)

```typescript
test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
  /**
   * Given: Eine aktive Session fГјr importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
   * Then: Bestehende Session zurГјckgegeben, keine neue erstellt
   */
})
```

### Tests als Dokumentation

Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`

---

## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)

| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
|--------------|-------------|
| Implementierungsdetails testen | PrГјfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | PrГјfe spezifische Werte |
| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo mГ¶glich |
| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | PrГјfe nur Schema-KonformitГӨt |
| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) fГјr Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |

---

## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |

---

## Detail-Module

| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
|-------|------|--------|
| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, AusfГјhrungsbefehle |
| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, hГӨufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |

---

> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Г„nderungen an Test-Praktiken mГјssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.

## S5 вҖ” Onboarding: Welcome Card for First-Time Visitors

**Files:** `apps/web/src/components/onboarding/welcome-card.tsx` (new), `apps/web/src/routes/home-page.tsx`

When there are no active imports and no recent jobs, show a welcome card instead of the empty field:

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  Conversations importieren und anpassen           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  FГјge einen Г¶ffentlichen Share-Link ein вҖ” zum     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Beispiel von ChatGPT oder Claude вҖ” und passe     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  das GesprГӨchsformat nach deinen WГјnschen an.     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  1. Link einfГјgen und importieren                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  2. Zwischen Ansichten wechseln                   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  3. Stellen markieren und anpassen lassen         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  [Probiere es aus вҶ’]  (scrolls to input)          в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

Requirements:
- Card only visible when `allJobs.length === 0`
- `localStorage` key `onboarding-dismissed` to permanently dismiss card
- Close button (X) top right
- "Probiere es aus вҶ’" button scrolls/focuses the URL input
- Use the existing `Card` component from `apps/web/src/components/ui/card.tsx`

Read `home-page.tsx` first to understand the current structure and where to integrate the welcome card.

## TDD
- Follow strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement, then refactor
- Read TESTING.md before writing tests (use Vitest + React Testing Library + jsdom)
- Test: welcome card renders when no jobs exist
- Test: welcome card doesn't render when jobs exist
- Test: dismiss button hides card and sets localStorage
- Commit your changes atomically when tests pass (stage files by name, never `git add .`)
- Commit message: `feat(web): add welcome card onboarding for first-time visitors`

## Frontend testing pattern
```typescript
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query"
import { render } from "@testing-library/react"

function createTestQueryClient() {
  return new QueryClient({
    defaultOptions: { queries: { retry: false }, mutations: { retry: false } },
  })
}

function renderWithProviders(ui: ReactNode) {
  const queryClient = createTestQueryClient()
  return render(
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>{ui}</QueryClientProvider>,
  )
}
```

Use native Read only as prerequisite before Edit/Write, or for images/PDFs. ## Working Directory /Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter ## S14 вҖ” Import Progress Bar **Files:** `apps/web/src/components/format-workspace/status-header.tsx` **Problem:** Currently only text + spinner. User doesn't know how far the import is. **Solution:** Mini progress bar below the stage label: - Map known stages to fixed order вҶ’ derive percentage - Visual: thin bar (2px) below the stage label - Color: `bg-primary` on `bg-muted` background - Stages: queued=0%, validating=10%, fetching=25%, extracting=45%, cleaning=60%, structuring=75%, rendering=90%, done=100% ```tsx
``` **IMPORTANT:** In Wave 1, semantic badge variants were added to `badge.tsx` and a `status-variant.ts` mapping was created. The status-header.tsx now uses `getStatusVariant()` from `./status-variant`. Read the current file to see the exact current state before making changes. Read `status-header.tsx` first to understand the current structure and add the progress bar in the right place. ## TDD - Follow strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement, then refactor - Read TESTING.md before writing tests (use Vitest) - Extract the stage-to-progress mapping as a testable function - Test the stage-to-progress mapping (each stage returns correct percentage) - Commit your changes atomically when tests pass (stage files by name, never `git add .`) - Commit message: `feat(web): add stage progress bar to import status header`

content.content:
# Testing Best Practices вҖ” Chat Exporter

> Verbindliche Referenz fГјr alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** вҖ” es enthГӨlt die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.

## Tech-Stack

| Bereich | Tool | Version |
|---------|------|---------|
| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | вҖ” |
| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |

## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing

**Lies nur die Module, die fГјr deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**

| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
|---------|-------------------|
| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
| **Code-Review durchfГјhren** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
| **Test-Kategorien wГӨhlen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
| **VerfГјgbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |

---

## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gГјltig)

### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung

Tests prГјfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.

```typescript
test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })

  const result = generatePreview(selection)

  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
})

test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
  generatePreview(createSelection())
  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
})
```

### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)

Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:

```typescript
test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }

  const result = createSelection(overrides)

  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
})
```

### Given-When-Then (fГјr komplexe Szenarien)

```typescript
test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
})
```

### Tests als Dokumentation

Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`

---

## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)

| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
|--------------|-------------|
| Implementierungsdetails testen | PrГјfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | PrГјfe spezifische Werte |
| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo mГ¶glich |
| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | PrГјfe nur Schema-KonformitГӨt |
| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) fГјr Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |

---

## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |

---

## Detail-Module

| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
|-------|------|--------|
| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, AusfГјhrungsbefehle |
| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, hГӨufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |

---

> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Г„nderungen an Test-Praktiken mГјssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.

# Frontend-Testing-Standards

> Detail-Referenz вҖ” geladen aus `TESTING.md` Index.

## Stack

| Komponente | Tool |
|------------|------|
| Test-Runner | **Vitest** |
| Umgebung | **jsdom** |
| Rendering | **React Testing Library** (`@testing-library/react`) |
| User-Events | **userEvent** (`@testing-library/user-event`) |
| State-Management | **TanStack Query** (via `createTestQueryClient()`) |

> **Status**: Web-App hat noch keine Unit-Tests. Dieses Modul definiert die Standards fГјr zukГјnftige Tests.

## Rendering-Pattern

Alle Komponenten-Tests mГјssen einen QueryClient-Wrapper verwenden:

```typescript
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query"
import { render } from "@testing-library/react"
import type { ReactNode } from "react"

function createTestQueryClient() {
  return new QueryClient({
    defaultOptions: {
      queries: { retry: false },
      mutations: { retry: false },
    },
  })
}

function renderWithProviders(ui: ReactNode) {
  const queryClient = createTestQueryClient()
  return render(
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>{ui}</QueryClientProvider>,
  )
}
```

```typescript
render(<FormatWorkspace />)
```

## User-Interaktionen

Immer `userEvent` statt `fireEvent` verwenden вҖ” nГӨher am realen Nutzerverhalten:

```typescript
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"

test("toggle switches adjustment mode", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup()
  renderWithProviders(<AdjustmentToggle />)

  await user.click(screen.getByRole("switch"))

  expect(screen.getByRole("switch")).toBeChecked()
})
```

## Async-Rendering abwarten

```typescript
import { screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"

test("loads and displays rules", async () => {
  renderWithProviders(<RulesList />)

  await waitFor(() =>
    expect(screen.getByText("Heading Rule")).toBeInTheDocument(),
  )
})
```

## Hook-Testing

TanStack Query Hooks mit `renderHook` testen:

```typescript
import { renderHook, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"

test("fetches adjustment session", async () => {
  const wrapper = ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (
    <QueryClientProvider client={createTestQueryClient()}>
      {children}
    </QueryClientProvider>
  )

  const { result } = renderHook(() => useAdjustmentSession("abc"), { wrapper })

  await waitFor(() => expect(result.current.isSuccess).toBe(true))
  expect(result.current.data?.rules).toHaveLength(2)
})
```

## Radix UI Polyfills

Komponenten die Radix UI nutzen (shadcn/ui), brauchen Browser-API-Polyfills:

```typescript
beforeAll(() => {
  Element.prototype.hasPointerCapture ??= vi.fn().mockReturnValue(false)
  Element.prototype.setPointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
  Element.prototype.releasePointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
  Element.prototype.scrollIntoView ??= vi.fn()
})
```

## Namenskonventionen

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Testdatei | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Testdatei (non-React) | `<module>.test.ts` | `format-utils.test.ts` |
| Test-Suite | `describe("<Component>", ...)` | `describe("RulesList", ...)` |
| Einzeltest | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("renders connected badge when active", ...)` |

1вҶ’import { cleanup, render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"
     2вҶ’import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"
     3вҶ’import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, test, vi } from "vitest"
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’import { WelcomeCard } from "./welcome-card"
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’describe("WelcomeCard", () => {
     8вҶ’  beforeEach(() => {
     9вҶ’    localStorage.clear()
    10вҶ’  })
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’  afterEach(() => {
    13вҶ’    cleanup()
    14вҶ’  })
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’  test("renders when visible is true and not previously dismissed", () => {
    17вҶ’    render(<WelcomeCard visible onScrollToInput={vi.fn()} />)
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’    expect(
    20вҶ’      screen.getByText("Conversations importieren und anpassen"),
    21вҶ’    ).toBeInTheDocument()
    22вҶ’    expect(screen.getByText(/Probiere es aus/)).toBeInTheDocument()
    23вҶ’  })
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’  test("does not render when visible is false", () => {
    26вҶ’    render(<WelcomeCard visible={false} onScrollToInput={vi.fn()} />)
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’    expect(
    29вҶ’      screen.queryByText("Conversations importieren und anpassen"),
    30вҶ’    ).not.toBeInTheDocument()
    31вҶ’  })
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’  test("does not render when previously dismissed via localStorage", () => {
    34вҶ’    localStorage.setItem("onboarding-dismissed", "true")
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’    render(<WelcomeCard visible onScrollToInput={vi.fn()} />)
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’    expect(
    39вҶ’      screen.queryByText("Conversations importieren und anpassen"),
    40вҶ’    ).not.toBeInTheDocument()
    41вҶ’  })
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’  test("dismiss button hides card and sets localStorage", async () => {
    44вҶ’    const user = userEvent.setup()
    45вҶ’    render(<WelcomeCard visible onScrollToInput={vi.fn()} />)
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’    await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: /schlieГҹen/i }))
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’    expect(
    50вҶ’      screen.queryByText("Conversations importieren und anpassen"),
    51вҶ’    ).not.toBeInTheDocument()
    52вҶ’    expect(localStorage.getItem("onboarding-dismissed")).toBe("true")
    53вҶ’  })
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’  test("action button calls onScrollToInput", async () => {
    56вҶ’    const user = userEvent.setup()
    57вҶ’    const onScrollToInput = vi.fn()
    58вҶ’    render(<WelcomeCard visible onScrollToInput={onScrollToInput} />)
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’    await user.click(screen.getByText(/Probiere es aus/))
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’    expect(onScrollToInput).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()
    63вҶ’  })
    64вҶ’})
    65вҶ’

   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’export function getAdjustViewLabel(view: ViewMode) {
   162вҶ’  return `${getViewLabel(view)} anpassen`;
   163вҶ’}
   164вҶ’
   165вҶ’export function getEndAdjustLabel() {
   166вҶ’  return adjustmentLabels.endAdjustMode;
   167вҶ’}
   168вҶ’
   169вҶ’// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   170вҶ’// View Labels
   171вҶ’// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   172вҶ’
   173вҶ’export function getViewLabel(view: ViewMode) {
   174вҶ’  return viewLabels[view];
   175вҶ’}
   176вҶ’
   177вҶ’export function getRoleLabel(role: string) {
   178вҶ’  return roleLabels[role] ?? role;
   179вҶ’}
   180вҶ’
   181вҶ’export function getBlockTypeLabel(blockType: string) {
   182вҶ’  return blockTypeLabels[blockType] ?? blockType;
   183вҶ’}
   184вҶ’
   185вҶ’export function getRuleKindLabel(kind: FormatRuleKind) {
   186вҶ’  return ruleKindLabels[kind];
   187вҶ’}
   188вҶ’
   189вҶ’// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   190вҶ’// Selection Formatter
   191вҶ’// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   192вҶ’
   193вҶ’export function formatMarkdownLinesLabel(start: number, end: number) {
   194вҶ’  return `Markdown-Zeilen ${start}-${end}`;
   195вҶ’}
   196вҶ’
   197вҶ’export function formatMessageBlockLabel(
   198вҶ’  role: string,
   199вҶ’  index: number,
   200вҶ’  blockType: string,
   201вҶ’) {
   202вҶ’  return `${getRoleLabel(role)}-Nachricht ${index} В· ${getBlockTypeLabel(blockType)}`;
   203вҶ’}
   204вҶ’
   205вҶ’// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   206вҶ’// Rules Labels
   207вҶ’// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   208вҶ’
   209вҶ’export const rulesLabels = {
   210вҶ’  activeRulesCount: (count: number) =>
   211вҶ’    count > 0 ? `${count} ${count === 1 ? "Regel" : "Regeln"} aktiv` : "Regeln",
   212вҶ’  noActiveRules: "Keine aktiven Regeln.",
   213вҶ’  allImports: "FГјr alle Imports",
   214вҶ’  thisImportOnly: "Nur dieser Import",
   215вҶ’  loading: "Wird geladen...",
   216вҶ’  rationale: "BegrГјndung",
   217вҶ’  defaultRationale:
   218вҶ’    "Diese Regel wurde aus einer frГјheren Anpassungssession fГјr diesen Import erzeugt.",
   219вҶ’  exactScopeNote: "Diese Regel gilt nur fГјr die ursprГјngliche Auswahl.",
   220вҶ’  globalScopeNote: "Diese Regel gilt fГјr alle Imports in diesem Format.",
   221вҶ’  undoPending: "Wird rГјckgГӨngig gemacht...",
   222вҶ’  undo: "RГјckgГӨngig",
   223вҶ’} as const;
   224вҶ’
   225вҶ’// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   226вҶ’// Loading & Misc Labels
   227вҶ’// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   228вҶ’
   229вҶ’export const miscLabels = {
   230вҶ’  transcriptLoading: "Transkript wird vorbereitet",
   231вҶ’  importFailed: "Import fehlgeschlagen",
   232вҶ’  errorInPhase: (phase: string) => `Fehler in Phase: ${phase}`,
   233вҶ’  viewLoadError: "Diese Ansicht konnte nicht geladen werden.",
   234вҶ’  retryButton: "Erneut versuchen",
   235вҶ’  adjustmentLoadError: "Anpassungen konnten nicht geladen werden.",
   236вҶ’} as const;
   237вҶ’
   238вҶ’export function getRuleLabel(rule: FormatRule) {
   239вҶ’  const summary = rule.instruction.trim();
   240вҶ’
   241вҶ’  if (summary.length <= 72) {
   242вҶ’    return summary;
   243вҶ’  }
   244вҶ’
   245вҶ’  return `${summary.slice(0, 69).trimEnd()}...`;
   246вҶ’}
   247вҶ’

feat(web): add stage progress bar to import status header

Extract stage-to-progress percentage mapping as testable getStageProgress()
function and render a thin animated progress bar below the stage label
during active imports. Maps each pipeline stage to a fixed percentage
(validate=10%, fetch=25%, extract=45%, normalize=60%, structure=75%,
render=90%, done=100%).

EOF

cd "/Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter" && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(web): add welcome card onboarding for first-time visitors

Show a welcome card when no imports exist, explaining the 3-step
workflow. Card is dismissible via X button (persisted in localStorage)
and includes a CTA that scrolls/focuses the URL input field.

Uses happy-dom for tests since jsdom has an ESM compatibility issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

refactor(web): simplify and de-jargon all UI labels

S7: Replace view-dependent getAdjustViewLabel()/getEndAdjustLabel() with
static adjustLabel ("Anpassen") and adjustDoneLabel ("Fertig") on the
adjustmentLabels object. Update all consumers (completed-toolbar,
adjustment-mode-guide).

S8: Shorten guideInstruction to a single actionable sentence and clear
guideNote (no longer needed).

S10: Replace technical jargon in ruleKindLabels (e.g. "Inline-Semantik"
-> "Textformatierung"), blockTypeLabels ("Markdown-Zeilen" ->
"Textzeilen"), and rulesLabels scope strings ("FГјr alle Imports" ->
"Гңberall anwenden", "Nur dieser Import" -> "Nur hier").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Completed status always returns 100%, failed status returns the progress of the stage where it failed.

The progress bar only shows when the job is not completed and has an active stage.

в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  Conversations importieren und anpassen           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  FГјge einen Г¶ffentlichen Share-Link ein вҖ” zum     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Beispiel von ChatGPT oder Claude вҖ” und passe     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  das GesprГӨchsformat nach deinen WГјnschen an.     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  1. Link einfГјgen und importieren                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  2. Zwischen Ansichten wechseln                   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  3. Stellen markieren und anpassen lassen         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  [Probiere es aus вҶ’]  (scrolls to input)          в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

Requirements:
- Card only visible when `allJobs.length === 0`
- `localStorage` key `onboarding-dismissed` to permanently dismiss card
- Close button (X) top right
- "Probiere es aus вҶ’" button scrolls/focuses the URL input
- Use the existing `Card` component from `apps/web/src/components/ui/card.tsx`

Read `home-page.tsx` first to understand the current structure and where to integrate the welcome card.

## TDD
- Follow strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement, then refactor
- Read TESTING.md before writing tests (use Vitest + React Testing Library + jsdom)
- Test: welcome card renders when no jobs exist
- Test: welcome card doesn't render when jobs exist
- Test: dismiss button hides card and sets localStorage
- Commit your changes atomically when tests pass (stage files by name, never `git add .`)
- Commit message: `feat(web): add welcome card onboarding for first-time visitors`

## Frontend testing pattern
```typescript
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query"
import { render } from "@testing-library/react"

function createTestQueryClient() {
  return new QueryClient({
    defaultOptions: { queries: { retry: false }, mutations: { retry: false } },
  })
}

function renderWithProviders(ui: ReactNode) {
  const queryClient = createTestQueryClient()
  return render(
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>{ui}</QueryClientProvider>,
  )
}
```

content.text:
Die Welcome-Card-Dateien (S5) sind im Progress-Bar-Commit `03b6bd9` mit drin вҖ” die wurden dort mit-committed wegen der Race Condition. Die Dateien sind also alle im Repo. Die stray `apps/web/src/components/onboarding/CLAUDE.md` kann ignoriert werden.

Alles clean. Starte Wave 3 вҖ” 4 Agents parallel.

## Wave 3 вҖ” 4 Agents parallel

- **Agent A**: S2 вҖ” Adjustment-Feedback (Toast)
- **Agent B**: S3 вҖ” Rule-Feedback (Toast)
- **Agent C**: S6 вҖ” Placeholder-Rotation
- **Agent D**: S9 вҖ” Toolbar Pill-Group

input.prompt:
You are implementing plan step S2 from a UX overhaul plan for a React/Vite app.

Use Bash commands (cat, rg, find, ls) for all file operations вҖ” never native Read/Grep/Glob tools.
Use native Read only as prerequisite before Edit/Write, or for images/PDFs.

## Working Directory
/Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter

## S2 вҖ” Feedback bei Adjustment-Aktionen (Apply / Discard)

**Files:** `apps/web/src/components/format-workspace/use-adjustment-session.ts`, `apps/web/src/components/format-workspace/adjustment-popover.tsx`

Add toasts for all adjustment actions (via `import { toast } from "sonner"`):

- **Apply successful:** `toast.success("Anpassung Гјbernommen")`
- **Discard successful:** `toast.info("Letzte Г„nderung verworfen")`
- **Apply failed:** `toast.error("Anpassung fehlgeschlagen")`

The existing inline error box in the popover stays вҖ” toast is additional, so the user gets feedback even when the popover is closed.

Read both files first to understand the current structure, then add toast calls at the appropriate success/error callbacks.

**IMPORTANT:** In Wave 2, the labels in these files were updated (S7 changed imports from `getAdjustViewLabel` to `adjustmentLabels.adjustLabel`). Read the current state of the files.

## TDD
- Follow strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement, then refactor
- Commit your changes atomically when tests pass (stage files by name, never `git add .`)
- Commit message: `feat(web): add toast feedback for adjustment apply/discard`

Note: The web test runner has a pre-existing jsdom ESM issue. If tests can't run due to this, use `// @vitest-environment node` or `// @vitest-environment happy-dom` directive. The `happy-dom` package was added as a dev dependency.
input.name: wave3-adj-toast

content.content:
# Testing Best Practices вҖ” Chat Exporter

> Verbindliche Referenz fГјr alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** вҖ” es enthГӨlt die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.

## Tech-Stack

| Bereich | Tool | Version |
|---------|------|---------|
| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | вҖ” |
| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |

## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing

**Lies nur die Module, die fГјr deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**

| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
|---------|-------------------|
| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
| **Code-Review durchfГјhren** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
| **Test-Kategorien wГӨhlen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
| **VerfГјgbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |

---

## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gГјltig)

### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung

Tests prГјfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.

```typescript
// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })

  const result = generatePreview(selection)

  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
})

// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
  generatePreview(createSelection())
  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
})
```

### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)

Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:

```typescript
test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
  // Arrange
  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }

  // Act
  const result = createSelection(overrides)

  // Assert
  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
})
```

### Given-When-Then (fГјr komplexe Szenarien)

```typescript
test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
  /**
   * Given: Eine aktive Session fГјr importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
   * Then: Bestehende Session zurГјckgegeben, keine neue erstellt
   */
})
```

### Tests als Dokumentation

Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`

---

## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)

| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
|--------------|-------------|
| Implementierungsdetails testen | PrГјfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | PrГјfe spezifische Werte |
| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo mГ¶glich |
| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | PrГјfe nur Schema-KonformitГӨt |
| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) fГјr Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |

---

## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |

---

## Detail-Module

| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
|-------|------|--------|
| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, AusfГјhrungsbefehle |
| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, hГӨufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |

---

> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Г„nderungen an Test-Praktiken mГјssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.

# Frontend-Testing-Standards

> Detail-Referenz вҖ” geladen aus `TESTING.md` Index.

## Stack

| Komponente | Tool |
|------------|------|
| Test-Runner | **Vitest** |
| Umgebung | **jsdom** |
| Rendering | **React Testing Library** (`@testing-library/react`) |
| User-Events | **userEvent** (`@testing-library/user-event`) |
| State-Management | **TanStack Query** (via `createTestQueryClient()`) |

> **Status**: Web-App hat noch keine Unit-Tests. Dieses Modul definiert die Standards fГјr zukГјnftige Tests.

## Rendering-Pattern

Alle Komponenten-Tests mГјssen einen QueryClient-Wrapper verwenden:

```typescript
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query"
import { render } from "@testing-library/react"
import type { ReactNode } from "react"

function createTestQueryClient() {
  return new QueryClient({
    defaultOptions: {
      queries: { retry: false },
      mutations: { retry: false },
    },
  })
}

function renderWithProviders(ui: ReactNode) {
  const queryClient = createTestQueryClient()
  return render(
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>{ui}</QueryClientProvider>,
  )
}
```

```typescript
// FALSCH: Ohne Provider вҖ” schlГӨgt fehl bei Komponenten mit Queries/Mutations
render(<FormatWorkspace />)
```

## User-Interaktionen

Immer `userEvent` statt `fireEvent` verwenden вҖ” nГӨher am realen Nutzerverhalten:

```typescript
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"

test("toggle switches adjustment mode", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup()
  renderWithProviders(<AdjustmentToggle />)

  await user.click(screen.getByRole("switch"))

  expect(screen.getByRole("switch")).toBeChecked()
})
```

## Async-Rendering abwarten

```typescript
import { screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"

test("loads and displays rules", async () => {
  renderWithProviders(<RulesList />)

  await waitFor(() =>
    expect(screen.getByText("Heading Rule")).toBeInTheDocument(),
  )
})
```

## Hook-Testing

TanStack Query Hooks mit `renderHook` testen:

```typescript
import { renderHook, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"

test("fetches adjustment session", async () => {
  const wrapper = ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (
    <QueryClientProvider client={createTestQueryClient()}>
      {children}
    </QueryClientProvider>
  )

  const { result } = renderHook(() => useAdjustmentSession("abc"), { wrapper })

  await waitFor(() => expect(result.current.isSuccess).toBe(true))
  expect(result.current.data?.rules).toHaveLength(2)
})
```

## Radix UI Polyfills

Komponenten die Radix UI nutzen (shadcn/ui), brauchen Browser-API-Polyfills:

```typescript
beforeAll(() => {
  Element.prototype.hasPointerCapture ??= vi.fn().mockReturnValue(false)
  Element.prototype.setPointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
  Element.prototype.releasePointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
  Element.prototype.scrollIntoView ??= vi.fn()
})
```

## Namenskonventionen

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Testdatei | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Testdatei (non-React) | `<module>.test.ts` | `format-utils.test.ts` |
| Test-Suite | `describe("<Component>", ...)` | `describe("RulesList", ...)` |
| Einzeltest | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("renders connected badge when active", ...)` |

Tests prГјfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.

```typescript

| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
|--------------|-------------|
| Implementierungsdetails testen | PrГјfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | PrГјfe spezifische Werte |

# Frontend-Testing-Standards

> Detail-Referenz вҖ” geladen aus `TESTING.md` Index.

## Stack

| Komponente | Tool |
|------------|------|
| Test-Runner | **Vitest** |
| Umgebung | **jsdom** |
| Rendering | **React Testing Library** (`@testing-library/react`) |
| User-Events | **userEvent** (`@testing-library/user-event`) |
| State-Management | **TanStack Query** (via `createTestQueryClient()`) |

> **Status**: Web-App hat noch keine Unit-Tests. Dieses Modul definiert die Standards fГјr zukГјnftige Tests.

## Rendering-Pattern

Alle Komponenten-Tests mГјssen einen QueryClient-Wrapper verwenden:

```typescript
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query"
import { render } from "@testing-library/react"
import type { ReactNode } from "react"

function createTestQueryClient() {
  return new QueryClient({
    defaultOptions: {
      queries: { retry: false },
      mutations: { retry: false },
    },
  })
}

function renderWithProviders(ui: ReactNode) {
  const queryClient = createTestQueryClient()
  return render(
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>{ui}</QueryClientProvider>,
  )
}
```

```typescript
// FALSCH: Ohne Provider вҖ” schlГӨgt fehl bei Komponenten mit Queries/Mutations
render(<FormatWorkspace />)
```

## User-Interaktionen

Immer `userEvent` statt `fireEvent` verwenden вҖ” nГӨher am realen Nutzerverhalten:

```typescript
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"

test("toggle switches adjustment mode", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup()
  renderWithProviders(<AdjustmentToggle />)

  await user.click(screen.getByRole("switch"))

  expect(screen.getByRole("switch")).toBeChecked()
})
```

## Async-Rendering abwarten

```typescript
import { screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"

test("loads and displays rules", async () => {
  renderWithProviders(<RulesList />)

  await waitFor(() =>
    expect(screen.getByText("Heading Rule")).toBeInTheDocument(),
  )
})
```

## Hook-Testing

TanStack Query Hooks mit `renderHook` testen:

```typescript
import { renderHook, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"

test("fetches adjustment session", async () => {
  const wrapper = ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (
    <QueryClientProvider client={createTestQueryClient()}>
      {children}
    </QueryClientProvider>
  )

  const { result } = renderHook(() => useAdjustmentSession("abc"), { wrapper })

  await waitFor(() => expect(result.current.isSuccess).toBe(true))
  expect(result.current.data?.rules).toHaveLength(2)
})
```

## Radix UI Polyfills

Komponenten die Radix UI nutzen (shadcn/ui), brauchen Browser-API-Polyfills:

```typescript
beforeAll(() => {
  Element.prototype.hasPointerCapture ??= vi.fn().mockReturnValue(false)
  Element.prototype.setPointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
  Element.prototype.releasePointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
  Element.prototype.scrollIntoView ??= vi.fn()
})
```

## Namenskonventionen

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Testdatei | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Testdatei (non-React) | `<module>.test.ts` | `format-utils.test.ts` |
| Test-Suite | `describe("<Component>", ...)` | `describe("RulesList", ...)` |
| Einzeltest | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("renders connected badge when active", ...)` |

You are implementing plan step S6 from a UX overhaul plan for a React/Vite app.

Use Bash commands (cat, rg, find, ls) for all file operations вҖ” never native Read/Grep/Glob tools.
Use native Read only as prerequisite before Edit/Write, or for images/PDFs.

## Working Directory
/Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter

## S6 вҖ” Placeholder-Verbesserung im URL-Input

**File:** `apps/web/src/routes/home-page.tsx`

**Current:** `"https://chatgpt.com/share/... oder ein anderer Г¶ffentlicher KI-Share-Link"`

**New:** Dynamic placeholder rotating between examples (every 4s):
- `"https://chatgpt.com/share/abc123..."`
- `"https://claude.ai/share/xyz789..."`

Implementation as `usePlaceholderRotation(examples, intervalMs)` hook. No flicker вҖ” smooth transition via `transition-opacity`.

- On focus: rotation stops
- On blur: rotation resumes

Read `home-page.tsx` first to find the current placeholder and understand where to integrate.

**IMPORTANT:** Wave 2 added a WelcomeCard and `urlInputRef` to home-page.tsx. Read the current state.

Create the hook in a separate file (e.g., `apps/web/src/hooks/use-placeholder-rotation.ts`) for testability.

## TDD
- Follow strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement, then refactor
- Test the hook: returns cycling values, stops on pause, resumes on unpause
- Use `vi.useFakeTimers()` for interval testing
- Commit your changes atomically when tests pass (stage files by name, never `git add .`)
- Commit message: `feat(web): add rotating placeholder examples for URL input`

Note: Use `// @vitest-environment happy-dom` or `// @vitest-environment node` directive if jsdom has ESM issues.

# Testing Best Practices вҖ” Chat Exporter

> Verbindliche Referenz fГјr alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** вҖ” es enthГӨlt die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.

## Tech-Stack

| Bereich | Tool | Version |
|---------|------|---------|
| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | вҖ” |
| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |

## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing

**Lies nur die Module, die fГјr deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**

| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
|---------|-------------------|
| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
| **Code-Review durchfГјhren** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
| **Test-Kategorien wГӨhlen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
| **VerfГјgbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |

---

## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gГјltig)

### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung

Tests prГјfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.

```typescript
test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })

  const result = generatePreview(selection)

  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
})

test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
  generatePreview(createSelection())
  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
})
```

### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)

Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:

```typescript
test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }

  const result = createSelection(overrides)

  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
})
```

### Given-When-Then (fГјr komplexe Szenarien)

```typescript
test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
})
```

### Tests als Dokumentation

Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`

---

## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)

| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
|--------------|-------------|
| Implementierungsdetails testen | PrГјfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | PrГјfe spezifische Werte |
| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo mГ¶glich |
| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | PrГјfe nur Schema-KonformitГӨt |
| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) fГјr Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |

---

## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |

---

## Detail-Module

| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
|-------|------|--------|
| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |

| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, AusfГјhrungsbefehle |
| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, hГӨufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |

---

|--------------|-------------|
| Implementierungsdetails testen | PrГјfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | PrГјfe spezifische Werte |
| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo mГ¶glich |
| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | PrГјfe nur Schema-KonformitГӨt |
| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) fГјr Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |

---

## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |

---

## Detail-Module

| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
|-------|------|--------|
| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, AusfГјhrungsbefehle |
| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, hГӨufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |

---

> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Г„nderungen an Test-Praktiken mГјssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.

content.content:
# Frontend-Testing-Standards

> Detail-Referenz вҖ” geladen aus `TESTING.md` Index.

## Stack

| Komponente | Tool |
|------------|------|
| Test-Runner | **Vitest** |
| Umgebung | **jsdom** |
| Rendering | **React Testing Library** (`@testing-library/react`) |
| User-Events | **userEvent** (`@testing-library/user-event`) |
| State-Management | **TanStack Query** (via `createTestQueryClient()`) |

> **Status**: Web-App hat noch keine Unit-Tests. Dieses Modul definiert die Standards fГјr zukГјnftige Tests.

## Rendering-Pattern

Alle Komponenten-Tests mГјssen einen QueryClient-Wrapper verwenden:

```typescript
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query"
import { render } from "@testing-library/react"
import type { ReactNode } from "react"

function createTestQueryClient() {
  return new QueryClient({
    defaultOptions: {
      queries: { retry: false },
      mutations: { retry: false },
    },
  })
}

function renderWithProviders(ui: ReactNode) {
  const queryClient = createTestQueryClient()
  return render(
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>{ui}</QueryClientProvider>,
  )
}
```

```typescript
// FALSCH: Ohne Provider вҖ” schlГӨgt fehl bei Komponenten mit Queries/Mutations
render(<FormatWorkspace />)
```

## User-Interaktionen

Immer `userEvent` statt `fireEvent` verwenden вҖ” nГӨher am realen Nutzerverhalten:

```typescript
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"

test("toggle switches adjustment mode", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup()
  renderWithProviders(<AdjustmentToggle />)

  await user.click(screen.getByRole("switch"))

  expect(screen.getByRole("switch")).toBeChecked()
})
```

## Async-Rendering abwarten

```typescript
import { screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"

test("loads and displays rules", async () => {
  renderWithProviders(<RulesList />)

  await waitFor(() =>
    expect(screen.getByText("Heading Rule")).toBeInTheDocument(),
  )
})
```

## Hook-Testing

TanStack Query Hooks mit `renderHook` testen:

```typescript
import { renderHook, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"

test("fetches adjustment session", async () => {
  const wrapper = ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (
    <QueryClientProvider client={createTestQueryClient()}>
      {children}
    </QueryClientProvider>
  )

  const { result } = renderHook(() => useAdjustmentSession("abc"), { wrapper })

  await waitFor(() => expect(result.current.isSuccess).toBe(true))
  expect(result.current.data?.rules).toHaveLength(2)
})
```

## Radix UI Polyfills

Komponenten die Radix UI nutzen (shadcn/ui), brauchen Browser-API-Polyfills:

```typescript
beforeAll(() => {
  Element.prototype.hasPointerCapture ??= vi.fn().mockReturnValue(false)
  Element.prototype.setPointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
  Element.prototype.releasePointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
  Element.prototype.scrollIntoView ??= vi.fn()
})
```

## Namenskonventionen

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Testdatei | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Testdatei (non-React) | `<module>.test.ts` | `format-utils.test.ts` |
| Test-Suite | `describe("<Component>", ...)` | `describe("RulesList", ...)` |
| Einzeltest | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("renders connected badge when active", ...)` |

receive feedback even when the popover is closed:
- Apply success: toast.success("Anpassung Гјbernommen")
- Apply error: toast.error("Anpassung fehlgeschlagen")
- Discard success: toast.info("Letzte Г„nderung verworfen")

an additional feedback channel.

EOF
)"

content.content:
# Testing Best Practices вҖ” Chat Exporter

> Verbindliche Referenz fГјr alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** вҖ” es enthГӨlt die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.

## Tech-Stack

| Bereich | Tool | Version |
|---------|------|---------|
| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | вҖ” |
| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |

## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing

**Lies nur die Module, die fГјr deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**

| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
|---------|-------------------|
| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
| **Code-Review durchfГјhren** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
| **Test-Kategorien wГӨhlen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
| **VerfГјgbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |

---

## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gГјltig)

### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung

Tests prГјfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.

```typescript
// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })

  const result = generatePreview(selection)

  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
})

// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
  generatePreview(createSelection())
  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
})
```

### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)

Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:

```typescript
test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
  // Arrange
  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }

  // Act
  const result = createSelection(overrides)

  // Assert
  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
})
```

### Given-When-Then (fГјr komplexe Szenarien)

```typescript
test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
  /**
   * Given: Eine aktive Session fГјr importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
   * Then: Bestehende Session zurГјckgegeben, keine neue erstellt
   */
})
```

### Tests als Dokumentation

Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`

---

## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)

| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
|--------------|-------------|
| Implementierungsdetails testen | PrГјfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | PrГјfe spezifische Werte |
| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo mГ¶glich |
| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | PrГјfe nur Schema-KonformitГӨt |
| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) fГјr Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |

---

## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |

---

## Detail-Module

| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
|-------|------|--------|
| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, AusfГјhrungsbefehle |
| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, hГӨufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |

---

> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Г„nderungen an Test-Praktiken mГјssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.

# Frontend-Testing-Standards

> Detail-Referenz вҖ” geladen aus `TESTING.md` Index.

## Stack

| Komponente | Tool |
|------------|------|
| Test-Runner | **Vitest** |
| Umgebung | **jsdom** |
| Rendering | **React Testing Library** (`@testing-library/react`) |
| User-Events | **userEvent** (`@testing-library/user-event`) |
| State-Management | **TanStack Query** (via `createTestQueryClient()`) |

> **Status**: Web-App hat noch keine Unit-Tests. Dieses Modul definiert die Standards fГјr zukГјnftige Tests.

## Rendering-Pattern

Alle Komponenten-Tests mГјssen einen QueryClient-Wrapper verwenden:

```typescript
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query"
import { render } from "@testing-library/react"
import type { ReactNode } from "react"

function createTestQueryClient() {
  return new QueryClient({
    defaultOptions: {
      queries: { retry: false },
      mutations: { retry: false },
    },
  })
}

function renderWithProviders(ui: ReactNode) {
  const queryClient = createTestQueryClient()
  return render(
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>{ui}</QueryClientProvider>,
  )
}
```

```typescript
// FALSCH: Ohne Provider вҖ” schlГӨgt fehl bei Komponenten mit Queries/Mutations
render(<FormatWorkspace />)
```

## User-Interaktionen

Immer `userEvent` statt `fireEvent` verwenden вҖ” nГӨher am realen Nutzerverhalten:

```typescript
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"

test("toggle switches adjustment mode", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup()
  renderWithProviders(<AdjustmentToggle />)

  await user.click(screen.getByRole("switch"))

  expect(screen.getByRole("switch")).toBeChecked()
})
```

## Async-Rendering abwarten

```typescript
import { screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"

test("loads and displays rules", async () => {
  renderWithProviders(<RulesList />)

  await waitFor(() =>
    expect(screen.getByText("Heading Rule")).toBeInTheDocument(),
  )
})
```

## Hook-Testing

TanStack Query Hooks mit `renderHook` testen:

```typescript
import { renderHook, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"

test("fetches adjustment session", async () => {
  const wrapper = ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (
    <QueryClientProvider client={createTestQueryClient()}>
      {children}
    </QueryClientProvider>
  )

  const { result } = renderHook(() => useAdjustmentSession("abc"), { wrapper })

  await waitFor(() => expect(result.current.isSuccess).toBe(true))
  expect(result.current.data?.rules).toHaveLength(2)
})
```

## Radix UI Polyfills

Komponenten die Radix UI nutzen (shadcn/ui), brauchen Browser-API-Polyfills:

```typescript
beforeAll(() => {
  Element.prototype.hasPointerCapture ??= vi.fn().mockReturnValue(false)
  Element.prototype.setPointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
  Element.prototype.releasePointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
  Element.prototype.scrollIntoView ??= vi.fn()
})
```

## Namenskonventionen

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Testdatei | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Testdatei (non-React) | `<module>.test.ts` | `format-utils.test.ts` |
| Test-Suite | `describe("<Component>", ...)` | `describe("RulesList", ...)` |
| Einzeltest | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("renders connected badge when active", ...)` |

# Testing Best Practices вҖ” Chat Exporter

> Verbindliche Referenz fГјr alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** вҖ” es enthГӨlt die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.

## Tech-Stack

| Bereich | Tool | Version |
|---------|------|---------|
| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | вҖ” |
| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |

## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing

**Lies nur die Module, die fГјr deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**

| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
|---------|-------------------|
| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
| **Code-Review durchfГјhren** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
| **Test-Kategorien wГӨhlen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
| **VerfГјgbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |

---

## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gГјltig)

### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung

Tests prГјfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.

```typescript
// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })

  const result = generatePreview(selection)

  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
})

// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
  generatePreview(createSelection())
  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
})
```

### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)

Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:

```typescript
test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
  // Arrange
  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }

  // Act
  const result = createSelection(overrides)

  // Assert
  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
})
```

### Given-When-Then (fГјr komplexe Szenarien)

```typescript
test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
  /**
   * Given: Eine aktive Session fГјr importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
   * Then: Bestehende Session zurГјckgegeben, keine neue erstellt
   */
})
```

### Tests als Dokumentation

Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`

---

## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)

| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
|--------------|-------------|
| Implementierungsdetails testen | PrГјfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | PrГјfe spezifische Werte |
| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo mГ¶glich |
| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | PrГјfe nur Schema-KonformitГӨt |
| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) fГјr Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |

---

## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |

---

## Detail-Module

| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
|-------|------|--------|
| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, AusfГјhrungsbefehle |
| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, hГӨufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |

---

> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Г„nderungen an Test-Praktiken mГјssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.

# Frontend-Testing-Standards

> Detail-Referenz вҖ” geladen aus `TESTING.md` Index.

## Stack

| Komponente | Tool |
|------------|------|
| Test-Runner | **Vitest** |
| Umgebung | **jsdom** |
| Rendering | **React Testing Library** (`@testing-library/react`) |
| User-Events | **userEvent** (`@testing-library/user-event`) |
| State-Management | **TanStack Query** (via `createTestQueryClient()`) |

> **Status**: Web-App hat noch keine Unit-Tests. Dieses Modul definiert die Standards fГјr zukГјnftige Tests.

## Rendering-Pattern

Alle Komponenten-Tests mГјssen einen QueryClient-Wrapper verwenden:

```typescript
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query"
import { render } from "@testing-library/react"
import type { ReactNode } from "react"

function createTestQueryClient() {
  return new QueryClient({
    defaultOptions: {
      queries: { retry: false },
      mutations: { retry: false },
    },
  })
}

function renderWithProviders(ui: ReactNode) {
  const queryClient = createTestQueryClient()
  return render(
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>{ui}</QueryClientProvider>,
  )
}
```

```typescript
// FALSCH: Ohne Provider вҖ” schlГӨgt fehl bei Komponenten mit Queries/Mutations
render(<FormatWorkspace />)
```

## User-Interaktionen

Immer `userEvent` statt `fireEvent` verwenden вҖ” nГӨher am realen Nutzerverhalten:

```typescript
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"

test("toggle switches adjustment mode", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup()
  renderWithProviders(<AdjustmentToggle />)

  await user.click(screen.getByRole("switch"))

  expect(screen.getByRole("switch")).toBeChecked()
})
```

## Async-Rendering abwarten

```typescript
import { screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"

test("loads and displays rules", async () => {
  renderWithProviders(<RulesList />)

  await waitFor(() =>
    expect(screen.getByText("Heading Rule")).toBeInTheDocument(),
  )
})
```

## Hook-Testing

TanStack Query Hooks mit `renderHook` testen:

```typescript
import { renderHook, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"

test("fetches adjustment session", async () => {
  const wrapper = ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (
    <QueryClientProvider client={createTestQueryClient()}>
      {children}
    </QueryClientProvider>
  )

  const { result } = renderHook(() => useAdjustmentSession("abc"), { wrapper })

  await waitFor(() => expect(result.current.isSuccess).toBe(true))
  expect(result.current.data?.rules).toHaveLength(2)
})
```

## Radix UI Polyfills

Komponenten die Radix UI nutzen (shadcn/ui), brauchen Browser-API-Polyfills:

```typescript
beforeAll(() => {
  Element.prototype.hasPointerCapture ??= vi.fn().mockReturnValue(false)
  Element.prototype.setPointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
  Element.prototype.releasePointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
  Element.prototype.scrollIntoView ??= vi.fn()
})
```

## Namenskonventionen

| Element | Format | Beispiel |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Testdatei | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
| Testdatei (non-React) | `<module>.test.ts` | `format-utils.test.ts` |
| Test-Suite | `describe("<Component>", ...)` | `describe("RulesList", ...)` |
| Einzeltest | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("renders connected badge when active", ...)` |
